entities.name: Cardanus
entities.description: A scholar who derides magic fables and identifies illusions in conjuration.

attributes.action: derideth these and such like fables

entities.name: Jesus Christ
entities.description: Central figure of Christianity, used in various charms and curses.

entities.name: Lucifer
entities.description: Conjured in a curse against thieves to be destroyed.

entities.name: Saint Michael
entities.description: Mentioned in a curse against thieves.

entities.name: Finding out a thief (with crystal)
entities.description: Using an olive oil cross on a crystal and a child to see the thief.

entities.name: Finding out a thief (with shears/sieve)
entities.description: Using shears and a sieve to identify a guilty person.

entities.name: Saint Adelbert's Curse
entities.description: A long curse against thieves involving excommunication.

entities.name: Preventing witches (horse shoe)
entities.description: Nailing a horse shoe to the inside of the threshold.

entities.name: Olive leaves/branch
entities.description: Used in rituals to find thieves or ward off spirits.

entities.name: Sulphur
entities.description: Used for purification and driving out spirits.

summary: This passage from Reginald Scot's 'Discoverie of Witchcraft' documents various charms, curses, and preventative measures against witchcraft and theft. It includes both ritualistic instructions (such as finding thieves using crystals or sieves) and skeptical critiques by figures like Cardanus who expose these practices as mere 'knavery'.
